name: Current corridor baseline
summary: Normal corridor operation using the latest collected speeds.
assumptions: Demand follows the current corridor level.
Speeds are calibrated from the latest collected readings when available.

name: Lane reduction near Saba Pasha, Gleem, and Stanley
summary: A partial closure reduces capacity around the Saba Pasha, Gleem, and Stanley area.
assumptions: The affected area is reduced to one usable lane.
Traffic demand stays at the current corridor level.
Vehicle speeds are reduced only inside the affected area.

name: Sidi Gaber event traffic surge
summary: A short event surge increases demand around Sidi Gaber, Cleopatra, and Sporting.
assumptions: More vehicles enter the corridor near Sidi Gaber during the same time window.
All lanes stay open, but movement becomes slower around the station and nearby districts.
The pressure is strongest from Sidi Gaber through Sporting.

name: Shatby to Raml curbside bottleneck
summary: Curbside stopping and loading reduce movement near Shatby, Azarita, and Raml.
assumptions: Short stops, loading, or local pickup activity reduce usable road space.
The issue affects the western end of the monitored corridor near Raml.
Demand is only slightly higher, but movement slows inside the affected area.

name: Managed detour and signal support
summary: The same disruption is paired with demand diversion and smoother movement through the affected area.
assumptions: Some trips are diverted before reaching the affected area.
Traffic control keeps vehicles moving more smoothly than the disruption-only case.
The affected area still has reduced capacity.
